- Throughput target: steady-state 400–600 QPS for embedding-heavy microservices; bursts to ~1k QPS during peak batch runs.

Cc: CareLink Ops <ops@carelink.health>
Date: Fri, 20 Mar 2026 07:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production errors — 429 flood since last night

Ava / team,

We're seeing a sharp spike in 429s starting ~01:30 UTC this morning after we moved a large cohort of traffic to the new enterprise tier. Impact: API calls from our ingestion pipeline are being rate-limited, causing downstream processing delays and timeouts for clinical syncs.

Quick details:
- Affected endpoints: /v1/generate and /v1/embeddings
- Sample org/project IDs: org_carelink_7 / proj-ingest-14
- Approx. 20% of requests returned 429, many followed by retries that compounding failures

Can you confirm whether any quota/tiering change rolled out today and whether you can temporarily raise limits while we stabilize? We're prepared to open a maintenance win...

Date: Fri, 27 Feb 2026 08:12:00 -0800
Subject: Checkout failing — 5xx/timeout spike impacting payments

Hi Ben,

We're seeing a sudden spike in checkout failures starting ~07:55 PST. Customers get intermittent 5xx and long timeouts (20s+); our front-end is retrying aggressively which is compounding traffic. Cart abandonment has climbed sharply.

Errors from our logs (sample):
- POST /v1/generate -> 502 Bad Gateway (ALB)
- downstream timeouts to api.redwood.example: context deadline exceeded

I've attached a short log excerpt (error_log_0227.txt). Our ops team also saw elevated DNS lookup times right before the errors started. Can you confirm whether you see a regional issue or any throttling on your side? We have a large promo running and this is customer-facing — need urgent help.

From: Michael Chen <michael@voltretail.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karla Ruiz <karla@voltretail.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Feb 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Latency spike — checkout API 09:05 PST (prod)

Dylan,

We observed a significant latency spike and elevated 5xx rates on our checkout flow this morning around 09:05 PST. Page load times jumped to ~1.8s for the generation call that usually completes in ~200ms, and customers saw errors intermittently. This impacted two of our largest stores and resulted in ~7% checkout abandonment for a 10-minute window.

Attached is our capture summary and a sample request id: req_20260218_0905_41. We need an incident summary and ETA for root cause and remediation — our execs are asking for formal RCA and any financial relief.

Thanks,
Michael Chen
Head of Engineering
VoltRetail Inc.

Date: 2026-02-14T09:12:00Z
Subject: sudden p95/p99 increase + token/s collapse on hosted endpoint

Dylan —

We started seeing a sharp regression on our production calls to the hosted text-generation endpoint late last night (around 2026-02-13T18:00Z). p95 and p99 latency jumped ~3x and our observed tokens-per-second (TPS) dropped by ~60%. Error rate spiked to ~1.7% on some routes. This directly impacted customer checkouts (session timeouts).

We suspect this correlates with Redwood's performance optimizations you mentioned last week (quant/profile updates). Can you confirm whether any kernel selection / quant rollout happened around the 02/13 evening window? Attached are request samples and traces.

We need an ETA for mitigation; this is live-impacting. Please escalate.

—Sam W.

Subject: Intermittent inference jitter impacting a subset of customers

Hello,

We wanted to let you know we are investigating intermittent increases in token-level latency (jitter) for a small percentage of hosted inference requests affecting endpoints in us-west-1. Early impact window: 07:50-08:00 PST. Symptoms: short, sporadic increases in p95/p99 (generation stall) and rare truncated responses.

What we're doing:
- Ops has opened an incident on the status page (https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/INC-20251201-jitter)
- Roll-forward of a routing policy to reduce fanout for affected poo...

1) Recap (short):
- Goal you shared: reduce per-query inference cost by ~40% while keeping 95th percentile latency under 150ms for your search rerank workload.
- Proposed approach: Redwood dedicated inference with KV-cache + intelligent batching and a mixed-precision quantized model variant.

Subject: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)

Team,

We need to lock a plan for the integration test harness that creates/destroys an ephemeral VPC (ENG-4860) and how we fund + isolate it. Right now the proposal in the PR is “run on every PR,” which I don’t think we can sustain.

Can you reply with:
1) Estimated runtime + $/run (rough is fine)
2) Recommended gating: per-PR vs nightly vs release tags

Subject: Escalation: rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage (Redwood Private)

Markus,

We’re preparing our next Redwood Private upgrade in our VPC environment and need written clarity on two items before we’ll schedule the maintenance window:

1) Rollback guarantees: if the upgrade fails (including DB migration issues), can we roll back to the prior version and what is the expected time to restore service? We need something more concrete than “best effort.”

2) Support coverage: we will be doing this in our standard maintenance window (Saturday 1:00–4:00am ET). We need to know if Redwood will have engineering/SRE coverage during the window, and what escalatio...

Question: when a guardrail triggers (e.g., p99 latency regression, error rate spike, cost/token blowup), do we automatically stop the experiment (“hard-stop”), or do we mark as degraded + notify and require operator action (“soft-stop”)?

Proposal (recommended):
1) Initial release (beta + GA): SOFT-STOP only
- System evaluates guardrails continuously and sets experiment.status = DEGRADED when triggered

We're getting close to ramping our user-facing assistant into production (Q1 2025) and need help sizing a Dedicated reservation th...
text:
From: Daniel Park <daniel@neurolabs.ai>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2024-11-12T09:15:00-08:00
Subject: Dedicated capacity inquiry — throughput & ramp

Hi Sean,

We're getting close to ramping our user-facing assistant into production (Q1 2025) and need help sizing a Dedicated reservation that keeps latency predictable while optimizing cost. High-level:
- Peak concurrent sessions: ~1,200 (with 1.2k tokens/session average during bursts)
- Steady-state qps: ~120 requests/minute; bursts up to ~1,200 requests/minute for short windows
- SLA target: p95 < 350ms on generation-only traffic; some calls will be multi-turn chat (KV cache matters)
- Geo: primarily US-west + some east coast redundancy later

Can you put together 2–3 concrete reservation options (cluster size, minimum reserved GPUs, burst capacity, recommended ramp schedule over 3–9 months) and a rough mont...

From: Alex Barnes <alex.barnes@finovo.com>

Date: Tue, 16 Apr 2024 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Thanks for the call — proposed next steps

Sean / Marissa — thanks again for the intro and the overview today. Finovo is very interested in a technical demo focused on: SSO integration, data residency options, and expected cost per 10k monthly requests.

Quick recap from our side:
- Primary use case: real-time document summarization for customer support
- P0 constraints: < 300ms p95 for short prompts, support for SSO (SAML), and SOC2 compliance
- Procurement / legal will want a high-level SOW and an initial pricing ballpark

Can we schedule a 60-minute technical demo next week? Our team is available:
- Wed Apr 24: 09:00–11:00 PT
- Thu Apr 25: 13:00–16:00 PT
